channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google just lost a major ad tech antitrust case. What happens next could rewire the web
item.description: Google has acted illegally to maintain a dominant position in online advertising, a federal judge ruled on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia watches its Trump overtures come to naught
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The AI starter pack trend is taking over LinkedIn and TikTok
item.description: What’s in your office starter pack? La Colombe cold brew and a New Yorker subscription? Bose headphones and Brooks Brothers? Thanks to the latest ChatGPT trend making the rounds, you can now find out.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Lawmakers launch investigation into 23andMe after bankruptcy
item.description: A group of House lawmakers launched a probe Thursday into 23andMe's handling of customers' sensitive data following the company's filing for bankruptcy last month. In a letter to 23andMe, House Commerce and Energy Committee Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) and Reps. Gus Bilirakis (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Roblox issued subpoena for potentially exposing children to graphic, harmful content
item.description: The attorney general expressed concern over the ability of predatory adults to freely send messages to minors.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: San Jose mayor Mahan, local congressman Liccardo lend support as Waymo applies to bring robotaxis to the city
item.description: Waymo initially faced a rocky road in bringing its pioneering technology to Bay Area streets and highways.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: He built an AI app to beat coding interviews. Then Columbia suspended him
item.description: A software application called Interview Coder promises to help software developers succeed at technical job interviews—by surreptitiously feeding them answers to programming questions via AI.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Seven rare earth metals that China is weaponizing against Trump’s tariffs
item.description: As geopolitical and trade tensions rise, China’s dominance of both mining and processing means these niche metals have become a cudgel to use against opponents.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Latest Instagram feature asks if you’re ready to reveal your innermost Reel personality to your besties
item.description: Instagram's latest feature lets you create a custom Reels feed in your DMs that appeals to you and your besties' interests.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Netflix reveals July 2025 launch and first trailer for The Sandman's second and final season, and it'll rise from its slumber in two parts
item.description: The Sandman season 2's dual release dates and first teaser have been unveiled amid the ongoing controversy surrounding Neil Gaiman.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Endangered sea turtle populations show signs of recovery in more than half the world, survey finds
item.description: A new global survey finds that endangered sea turtles show signs of recovery in a majority of places where they’re found worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge rules Google has illegal ad tech monopoly
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that Google illegally acquired and maintained a monopoly over advertising technology, the second time in less than a year that the tech giant has been found in violation of antitrust laws. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Many Americans share mistrust of artificial intelligence: Survey
item.description: Three-quarters of Americans say they trust information generated by artificial intelligence (AI) only sometimes or hardly ever, according to a recent survey.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: A restaurant in Havana defies Cuba's tech struggles with digital menus and a robot waitress
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — For Sonia Pérez, stepping into Havana’s Doña Alicia restaurant felt like entering a movie.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Why Elon Musk installed his top lieutenants at a federal agency you probably haven’t heard of
item.description: The GSA's acting administrator is a Silicon Valley tech executive with expertise in rolling out artificial intelligence tools and a wife who once worked for Musk at his social media company, X.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: CIOs must mind their own data confidence gap
item.description: As far as many C-suite business and IT executives are concerned, their company data is in great shape, capable of fueling data-driven decision-making and delivering AI-powered solutions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Zuckerberg once floated spinning off Instagram over antitrust fears, email reveals in trial
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zuckerberg Testifies Meta Faces Stiff Competition from TikTok
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized Wednesday that his social media company faces stiff competition from TikTok, as Meta seeks to fend off accusations from the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) that it has a monopoly over social networking.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI brings order to observability disorder
item.description: Digital tools are the lifeblood of today’s enterprises, but the complexity of hybrid cloud architectures, involving thousands of containers, microservices and applications frustrates operational leaders trying to optimize business outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system may orbit two failed stars, scientists say
item.description: Scientists may have found a new Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system that orbits two failed stars.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AMD sees $800 million charge from export curbs on AI chips
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. said it expects to take a charge of as much as $800 million after the Trump administration put new restrictions on semiconductor exports to China.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cato Networks augments CASB with genAI security
item.description: Cato Networks recently unveiled new generative AI capabilities in its Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that the secure access service edge (SASE) provider says will let enterprise IT organizations detect, analyze, and gain insights into the use of genAI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China Accuses US Operatives of Cyberattacks During Asian Games | AP News
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Smarter dairy farms where robots milk the cows
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how robots can milk, feed and clean cows on dairy farms, boosting efficiency and comfort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: Use APIs to understand cloud vulnerabilities, recommends the public sector solutions engineering lead for Wiz. The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: Wiz’s Chris Saunders on extending ZTA to cloud first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: More than $35,000 recovered in stolen Bitcoin scam for Ohio woman
item.description: An Ohio woman who fell victim to a cryptocurrency scam has been refunded most of the nearly $42,000 in stolen Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Keysight tools tackle data center deployment efficiency
item.description: Test and performance measurement vendor Keysight Technologies has developed Keysight Artificial Intelligence (KAI) to identify performance inhibitors affecting large GPU deployments. It emulates workload profiles, rather than using actual resources, to pinpoint performance bottlenecks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DeepSeek’s open source movement
item.description: DeepSeek may have originated in China, but it stopped being Chinese the minute it was released on Hugging Face with an accompanying paper detailing its development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World
